title: Events Funnel Analysis
goal: Analyze user events as a simple funnel: understand event types, unique users per step, conversion between steps, and where the biggest drop appears.
business_context: A product team wants to understand how users move from product interaction to orders and which event step deserves deeper investigation.

related_portfolio_templates.title: Events funnel case study
related_portfolio_templates.what_to_write: Define the funnel steps, show unique users and conversion rates, explain the largest drop, and document assumptions about event semantics.
related_portfolio_templates.chart_or_table: Include a funnel table with users/conversion and one bar chart of unique users by step.
related_portfolio_templates.readme_bullet: Analyzed product event data as a funnel, calculating unique-user conversion rates and identifying the highest-drop step with documented assumptions.

milestones.title: Map event types
milestones.description: Load df_events.csv, count event types, and decide which event types can form a useful funnel.
dataset_hints: Use df_events.csv and inspect event_type values.
milestones.starter_code:
import pandas as pd

events = pd.read_csv("datasets/df_events.csv")
print(events.shape)
print(events["event_type"].value_counts())
print(events.head())
checklist: I selected a plausible funnel order.
I noted any ambiguous event meanings.
milestones.portfolio_output: Event type map and chosen funnel definition.

milestones.title: Count unique users by step
milestones.description: Calculate unique users for each selected funnel step. Use unique users, not raw event count, unless you explicitly explain why.
dataset_hints: Use user_id as the user denominator.
milestones.starter_code:
funnel = (
    events.groupby("event_type", as_index=False)
    .agg(users=("user_id", "nunique"), events=("event_type", "size"))
    .sort_values("users", ascending=False)
)
print(funnel)
checklist: I used unique users for funnel steps.
I kept raw event counts as supporting context.
milestones.portfolio_output: Funnel table with users and events.

milestones.title: Calculate conversion rates
milestones.description: Choose a funnel order and calculate step-to-step conversion rates. If event meanings are unclear, document assumptions.
milestones.starter_code:
steps = funnel[["event_type", "users"]].copy()
steps["previous_users"] = steps["users"].shift(1)
steps["step_conversion"] = steps["users"] / steps["previous_users"]
print(steps)

milestones.title: Visualize the funnel
milestones.description: Build a simple bar chart showing users by funnel step. The chart should be readable and not oversell causal conclusions.
dataset_hints: Use the funnel table from previous milestones.
milestones.starter_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ax = steps.plot(x="event_type", y="users", kind="bar", legend=False, figsize=(8, 4))
ax.set_title("Users by event type")
ax.set_xlabel("Event type")
ax.set_ylabel("Unique users")
plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
checklist: I wrote one insight and one limitation.

milestones.title: Write the funnel summary
milestones.description: Summarize the biggest drop, limitations of event definitions, and the next data question to ask.
dataset_hints: Mention whether order data should be joined next.
checklist: I suggested one next analysis step.

deliverables: Written summary with assumptions and next step
portfolio_prompt: Write a funnel analysis case study: define the funnel, show user counts/conversions, explain the biggest drop, and state what data you need to validate the interpretation.
